_meta.description: Project lifecycle stages with active checklist item subsets and weight redistribution.
_meta.usage: Select the matching stage to reduce audit scope. Items not in the active set are skipped. Weights are redistributed across active dimensions only.

bootstrap.name: Bootstrap / Prototype
bootstrap.description: New project, exploring the idea. Under ~2k LOC, small team or solo.

rationale.1.1: Any stage needs an entry point for agents
rationale.2.1: CI is a core foundation even for prototypes
rationale.2.2: Linting catches the most common agent mistakes
rationale.2.4: Type safety is the single highest-ROI constraint
rationale.4.1: Basic test suite prevents regression from day one
rationale.4.2: Tests must block merges to be effective
rationale.5.1: Knowledge should live in-repo, not in chat
rationale.7.4: Environment recovery enables reproducible dev setup
rationale.8.1: Security baseline: never use admin credentials

growth.name: Growth / Active Development
growth.description: Building features, growing team. ~2k-50k LOC, architecture stabilizing.

rationale.skipped_1.5: ADRs and design docs gain value only at maturity
rationale.skipped_2.6: Remediation-aware errors are an advanced optimization
rationale.skipped_3.2_3.3_3.4: Advanced observability can wait until mature stage
rationale.skipped_4.4_4.6: Formalized done criteria and flake management come with scale
rationale.skipped_5.3_5.4: Machine-readable refs and tech composability are mature concerns
rationale.skipped_6.2_6.3: Systematic cleanup and debt tracking need codebase scale
rationale.skipped_7.3_7.6: Handoff bridges and durable execution are advanced patterns
rationale.skipped_8.2_8.5_8.6: Audit logging, security marking, and protocol trust are mature concerns

mature.name: Mature / Maintenance
mature.description: Stable product in production. 50k+ LOC, established team and processes.

rationale.all: All 44 items apply. Use default weights or project-type profile overrides.
